description: Sample resume data for a senior data scientist, demonstrating complete structured format with emphasis on ML/AI projects

resume_data.summary: Senior Data Scientist with 7+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning systems at scale. Ph.D. in Statistics with 12+ publications in top-tier venues (NeurIPS, ICML, KDD). Expert in deep learning, NLP, and recommendation systems. Led ML initiatives generating $15M+ in measurable business impact across e-commerce and fintech domains.

work_experience.title: Senior Data Scientist
work_experience.description:
Lead ML Scientist for Personalization team, serving 300M+ customers globally.
Designed and deployed deep learning recommendation model improving click-through rate by 23% and revenue by $8M/year.
Built real-time feature engineering pipeline processing 1M+ events/second with <50ms latency.
Led team of 5 data scientists; established model monitoring and A/B testing framework adopted across 3 teams.
Reduced model training cost by 60% through distributed training optimization on AWS SageMaker.

work_experience.title: Data Scientist
work_experience.description:
Built ML models for fraud detection and risk assessment in payment processing platform.
Developed gradient-boosted tree model reducing false positive rate by 35% while maintaining 99.7% fraud detection rate.
Implemented NLP-based merchant categorization system covering 50+ business categories with 94% accuracy.
Created anomaly detection system for payment patterns, preventing $3M+ in potential fraud losses.
Collaborated with engineering to productionize models serving 100M+ API requests/day.

work_experience.title: Research Scientist Intern
work_experience.description:
Conducted research on graph neural networks for social network analysis.
Developed novel GNN architecture achieving state-of-the-art on 3 benchmark datasets.
Published findings at NeurIPS 2019 (first author).

projects.name: GraphSAGE-Next
projects.description: Next-generation inductive graph representation learning framework. 2,000+ citations, used in production at multiple companies.

projects.name: FairML Toolkit
projects.description: Open-source toolkit for fairness auditing and bias mitigation in ML models. Integrated with scikit-learn API.

publications.title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs
Fairness-Aware Learning with Restricted Black-Box Access
Scalable Causal Inference for Online Experimentation
